content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 12, 2012

Meredith Mertens
Sales & Marketing Associate
Northern Lights Capital Group
44 Cook Street Suite 1020
Denver, CO 80206

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-12-085

Dear Ms. Mertens:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ On May 24, 2012, I gave you advice over the phone about the lobbyist ethics course requirement and in light of the phone advice you stated that you will not need written advice. We are, therefore, withdrawing your request for advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

HPW:jgl

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding lobbyist ethics course requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is withdrawing the written advice request because phone advice was already provided on May 24, 2012, and the requestor indicated they would not need written advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Phone advice was provided on May 24, 2012, and the requestor indicated no need for written advice, leading to the withdrawal. Document references Government Code sections 81000-91014 and California Code of Regulations sections 18110-18997.

QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding lobbyist ethics course requirements.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is withdrawing the written advice request because phone advice was already provided on May 24, 2012, and the requestor indicated they would not need written advice.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter from the FPPC dated June 12, 2012, confirming that a request for written advice regarding lobbying provisions and ethics course requirements has been withdrawn after phone advice was already provided.
